document.diagnosis: Iteration 22 again makes logic the weakest family: every reviewed system reached only 1/4 logic exact, despite the nine-bank oracle covering 2/4 logic cases. Selection therefore remains a bottleneck. The nine-bank one-review mechanism led at 7/12 by making five helpful and zero harmful interventions; the five-bank three-review champion reached 6/12 with four helpful and zero harmful interventions. Regenerate-then-repair reached 5/12 and increased oracle coverage from 5/12 to 7/12 by introducing two correct candidates, but failed to select both covered answers. Across seven pipeline panels, it is now 36/90 exact, with 48/90 expanded-oracle coverage versus 37/90 base-oracle coverage, 26 helpful interventions, and three harmful reversals. The next batch should therefore retain both repair allocations, replicate regeneration unchanged, and use sequential cross-examination as the orthogonal selector architecture.
most_important_evidence: Nine-bank one-review repair won Iteration 22 at 7/12, improving a 2/12 base plurality through five helpful interventions and no harmful reversals.
Five-bank three-review repair remained competitive at 6/12, with four helpful and zero harmful interventions, preserving its value as the pooled champion.
Regenerate-then-repair generated two newly correct candidates and raised coverage from 5/12 to 7/12, but converted only 5/12; candidate integration remains unresolved.
Logic was only 1/4 for every reviewed strategy, while the nine-bank oracle was 2/4, directly exposing a domain-neutral selection gap.
The proposed six-strategy batch has a worst-case cost of 240 calls: 108 shared base-bank calls plus 132 review or regeneration calls, below the 300-call cap.

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the minimum-cost independent baseline for panel calibration and marginal-call comparisons.
proposals.name: Direct One

proposals.hypothesis: Retain deterministic five-answer plurality as the shared-bank control for measuring the net value of every intervention.
proposals.name: Five-Vote Plurality

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the pooled champion unchanged; its replicated parallel repair panel remains the principal control for answer-generating falsification.
proposals.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate the current winner unchanged to test whether allocating calls to broader independent coverage and one decisive reviewer continues to avoid multi-reviewer dilution.
proposals.name: One-Review Nine-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate the frozen pipeline unchanged because regeneration continues to add correct candidates and its pooled helpful-to-harmful intervention balance remains strongly positive; the test is whether the terminal integrator can close more of the expanded-oracle gap.
proposals.name: Blind Regeneration then Falsifying Integration

proposals.hypothesis: Sequential falsification may improve candidate integration by forcing each reviewer to attack one explicit predecessor proposal, providing an orthogonal test against independent review plurality without increasing the champion's call count.
proposals.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination

research_hypotheses: The Iteration 22 lead of nine-bank one-review repair reflects a real allocation advantage: broader candidate coverage plus one reviewer is more reliable than three independent reviewers over five bases.
Blind regeneration has a reproducible domain-neutral coverage benefit, but final accuracy is constrained primarily by integration rather than generation.
Sequential cross-examination can reduce selector variance by making decisive claims explicit and falsifiable across stages.
Logic weakness will improve only if selection or repair converts already-covered candidates; merely enlarging the bank is insufficient.
document.risk_note: All results remain noisy development evidence from small fresh panels, and the advanced systems shared a 25% weakest-family result in Iteration 22. The pipeline's 12-answer expanded-oracle gap and three pooled harmful reversals remain material. No mechanism should be called general until it wins unchanged on larger sealed panels and untouched exact-verifiable families.
